title: National Strategy for Monitoring Antimicrobial-Resistant Salmonella Strains
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Antimicrobial-resistant   (AMR)   Salmonella     represents     a     critical     public     health     challenge  ,   causing     foodborne     illnesses     that   are   harder     to     treat     and     increasing     the     burden     on     healthcare     systems  .   A     national     monitoring     strategy     is     essential     to     track   AMR   Salmonella     trends  ,   identify     emerging     resistance     patterns  ,   and     guide     evidence-based     interventions  .   This     strategy     integrates     surveillance     across     farms  ,   food     processing     plants  ,   and     healthcare     facilities     to     capture     a     comprehensive     picture     of     resistance     pathways  . Data   sharing     between     regulatory     agencies  ,   laboratories  ,   and     industry     stakeholders     will     strengthen     outbreak     response     and     containment     efforts  . By   combining     molecular     tools  ,   epidemiological   data,   and     policy     enforcement  ,   a     robust     monitoring     system     will     help     reduce     transmission  ,   inform     prudent     antimicrobial     use   in   agriculture  ,   and     safeguard     public     health  .
